evidence: cloud and hybrid cloud solutions and artificial intelligence technologies, along with other digital transformation initiatives, will remain significant drivers of demand for data center infrastructure
we estimate that our land and other space held for, or actively under, construction could accommodate over 3,500 megawatts of additional data center capacity
we had 769 megawatts of projects underway across multiple metropolitan areas around the world, and 64% of this data center activity was pre-leased
The Internet of Things, 5G, autonomous vehicles and artificial intelligence, among other technological advancements, are driving this digital transformation
PlatformDIGITAL brings together users, networks, clouds, controls and systems to the data, removing barriers, creating centers of data exchange to accommodate distributed workflows
ServiceFabric offers virtual network orchestration to 305+ cloud on-ramps and 700 data centers globally to enable quick, secure, and reliable private connections
As artificial intelligence becomes more prevalent in the workplace, it may present new considerations that could affect our business and operating results
threat actors are increasingly sophisticated and using tools such as AI that circumvent controls and evade detection, making detection, mitigation and recovery challenging and uncertain
We may not be able to adapt to changing technologies and customer requirements, and our data center infrastructure may become obsolete

capex_mentions.quote: 769 megawatts of projects underway across multiple metropolitan areas around the world
capex_mentions.context: Active data center development pipeline as of December 31, 2025, 64% pre-leased

capex_mentions.quote: over 3,500 megawatts of additional data center capacity, including more than 1,000 additional megawatts developable in Northern Virginia
capex_mentions.context: Estimated future development capacity from land and space held for construction

capex_mentions.quote: raised over $3 billion of equity commitments that can support approximately $10 billion of total data center investment
capex_mentions.context: Digital DC Partners NA Fund capital raise for data center investment

limitations: AI investment intensity scored 3 rather than 4-5: capex figures cited are for general data center development, not explicitly AI-attributable; AI is named as a demand driver but no dollar amount is directly tied to AI-specific build-out or R&D

AI narrative concreteness scored 3: PlatformDIGITAL and ServiceFabric are named platforms but described in terms of connectivity/colocation capabilities rather than AI-specific deployed use cases with metrics; AI is primarily framed as a customer demand driver, not a DLR-operated AI product

AI risk disclosure scored 3: risk factors name AI-specific categories (AI-enabled threat actors, AI workplace impact) but do not provide quantified risk scenarios or named governance/oversight bodies for AI risk; mechanisms are described at a general level
